description.p: wt keeps every worktree of a git repository in one predictable place — a sibling directory named repo.worktrees — and makes creating, renaming and removing worktrees painless.

Removing a worktree never deletes its branch, and wt says so at every step. Uncommitted changes are listed before removal with an explicit choice: stash them (recoverable later) or discard them. Worktrees created outside the convention with plain git are detected and can be moved into place with one click.
Fix the stray-worktree banner showing even when every worktree is in place.
